summary: NIMA underperformance is primarily caused by a double-rescaling bug and model collapse due to uncalibrated input range, exacerbated by incomplete training and preprocessing mismatches.

double_rescaling.evidence: Project code passes [0, 1] images to EfficientNetV2B0 which includes an internal Rescaling(1/255.0) layer. Model effectively sees images near zero brightness.

model_collapse.evidence: Predicted positive ratio 96.8% vs GT 70.2%. Prediction range narrow (std 0.33 vs 0.69). Accuracy matches majority class baseline.

incomplete_training.evidence: Training was interrupted by a crash. Restart run only completed 4 epochs. Total training significantly below paper requirements.

preprocessing_shift.evidence: Training uses random_crop (maintains aspect ratio); Evaluation uses direct resize (warps image).

checkpoint_mismatch.evidence: final_model.keras and best.weights.h5 differ by 0.30 in mean score, suggesting restore_best_weights might have failed or been bypassed.
